content.problemStatement:
For an array AAA of length NNN, we define f(A)f(A)f(A) as follows:


Let SSS denote the sum so far, and HHH denote your happiness.

Initially, SSS and HHH are both 000.
Then, for each iii from 111 to NNN in order:

If SSS doesn't double upon adding AiA_iAi​ to it, add 111 to your happiness.

That is, if S+Ai≠2SS + A_i \ne 2SS+Ai​=2S, add 111 to HHH.

Note that if S+Ai=2SS + A_i = 2SS+Ai​=2S holds instead, HHH doesn't change.
Then, add AiA_iAi​ to SSS.


f(A)f(A)f(A) is then defined to be the final value of HHH.

To see an example of f(A)f(A)f(A) being computed, see the samples below.

You are given an array A=[A1,A2,…,AN]A = [A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N]A=[A1​,A2​,…,AN​] of length NNN.

You are allowed to freely rearrange this array however you like.

Find any rearrangement of AAA that **maximizes** f(A)f(A)f(A).

If there are multiple rearrangements that maximize f(A)f(A)f(A), any of them will be accepted.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN space-separated integers denoting the rearrangement of AAA that you found which maximizes f(A)f(A)f(A).


If there are multiple possible rearrangements, any of them will be accepted.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only possible rearrangement is [6][6][6].
Test case 222: Consider the rearrangement A=[2,3,1]A = [2, 3, 1]A=[2,3,1]. f(A)f(A)f(A) is computed as follows:

Initially, S=0S = 0S=0 and H=0H = 0H=0.
i=1i = 1i=1: Since S+A1=2≠2S=0S+A_1 = 2 \ne 2S = 0S+A1​=2=2S=0, we add 111 to HHH. Then we add A1A_1A1​ to SSS.
Now, S=2S = 2S=2 and H=1H = 1H=1.
i=2i = 2i=2: Since S+A2=5≠2S=4S+A_2 = 5 \ne 2S = 4S+A2​=5=2S=4, we add 111 to HHH. Then we add A2A_2A2​ to SSS.
Now, S=5S = 5S=5 and H=2H = 2H=2.
i=3i = 3i=3: Since S+A3=6≠2S=10S+A_3 = 6 \ne 2S = 10S+A3​=6=2S=10, we add 111 to HHH. Then we add A3A_3A3​ to SSS.
Now, S=6S = 6S=6 and H=3H = 3H=3.
The final value of f(A)f(A)f(A) is thus H=3H= 3H=3.
It can be proved that this is optimal.
